rationale: The cited paper is used in the citing article's core framing of effectiveness measurement: Danaher and Dagger explicitly state that Sethuraman, Tellis, and Briesch (2011) highlight advertising elasticity as the relevant sales-based criterion. It is also cited when motivating the value of single-source data and again in results discussion to benchmark their estimated TV and newspaper elasticities against meta-analytic norms from Sethuraman et al. Because the citation helps justify the chosen outcome metric and interpret empirical magnitudes, it plays a meaningful role beyond a passing literature-list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Sethuraman et al. (2011) as an empirical benchmark and conceptual anchor for evaluating advertising effectiveness via elasticity. It compares its channel-level elasticity estimates to the meta-analytic generalizations in the cited study to contextualize findings.
